capability_objective: Cancel the reservation “Cedar Room workshop” (ID hos-163) with reason “no longer needed for the scheduled work.” The first cancel call may time out after reaching the service. Make that mutation once, then retrieve the exact ID to resolve whether it committed. Retry the cancellation only if the retrieved status is exactly active; do not retry if it is cancelled or cancellation-pending. Do not search for or touch the similarly named distractor. Do not infer success from the timeout or from the initial fixture.
prompt:
The hospitality desk needs one reservation cancellation resolved safely despite an uncertain first response. Work only in the supplied repository. Read `HOSPITALITY.md`, then use Pi's genuine shell tool to run the local `./hospitalityctl` executable for every hospitality-system action. Run its built-in help before the first data action. Do not inspect or alter the executable, protected files, backing state, lock, or execution journal; do not access reservation facts by any route other than the executable.

Cancel the reservation named “Cedar Room workshop” with exact ID `hos-163`, recording the reason “no longer needed for the scheduled work.” Submit that cancellation once. The first request may reach the service and then report a timeout with its commit outcome unknown, so neither assume that it succeeded nor immediately repeat it. After that attempt finishes, retrieve `hos-163` directly and use the returned full record to resolve the uncertainty. Retry the same cancellation, with the same requested reason, only if that retrieval reports the status exactly as `active`. Do not retry when it reports `cancelled`, `cancellation-pending`, or any other value. If a retry is permitted, use its returned status as the final status; otherwise use the exact-ID retrieval's status.

Do not search or list reservations, and do not read, retrieve, cancel, or otherwise touch any similarly named record. Do not create, update, or notify anything. When the workflow is complete, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. Then respond with exactly three plain-text lines and no bullets: `Reservation: <returned name> (<returned ID>)`, `Verified final status: <grounded status>`, and `Cancellation retry: <performed or not performed>`. Ground every filled value in the exact-ID retrieval or a permitted retry result, never in repository fixtures or the timeout itself.
